volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 5.4.907b - Section 5.4.946b)

sections.label: Section 5.4.907b
sections.translation: So it has happened . But is this Simo ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.908
sections.translation: It is he .

sections.label: Section 5.4.908b
sections.original: Men quaeris ? eho tu Glycerium hinc civem esse ais ?
sections.translation: How now , do you say that Glycerium is a citizen of this place ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.909
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.909b
sections.translation: Have you come here so well prepared ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.909c
sections.translation: For what purpose ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.909d
sections.translation: Do you ask ? Are you to be acting this way with impunity ? Are you to be luring young men into snares here , inexperienced in affairs , and liberally brought up , by tempting them , and to be playing upon their fancies by making promises ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.912b
sections.translation: Are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.913
sections.translation: And are you to be patching up amours with Courtesans by marriage ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.914
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! I fear that the stranger will not put up with this .

sections.label: Section 5.4.914b
sections.translation: If , Simo , you knew this person well ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.915
sections.original: Non ita arbitrere : bonus est hic vir .
sections.translation: you would not think thus ; he is a worthy man .

sections.label: Section 5.4.915b
sections.translation: He , a worthy man ! To come so opportunely to-day just at the very nuptials , and yet never to have come before ? ( Ironically .) Of course , we must believe him , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.4.918
sections.original: Ni metuam patrem , habeo pro illa re illum quod moneam probe .
sections.translation: If I didn ’ t dread my father , I have something , which , in this conjuncture , I could opportunely suggest to him [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.919
sections.translation: A sharper ! [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.919b
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 5.4.919c
sections.translation: It is his way , Crito ; do excuse it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.919d
sections.translation: Let him take heed how he behaves .

sections.label: Section 5.4.920
sections.original: Si mihi pergit quae volt dicere , ea quae non volt audiet .
sections.translation: If he persists in saying to me what he likes , he ’ ll be hearing things that he don ’ t like . Am I meddling with these matters or interesting myself ? Can you not endure your troubles with a patient mind ? For as to what I say , whether it is true or false what I have heard , can soon be known . A certain man of Attica , a long time ago , [*] his ship being wrecked , was cast ashore at Andros , and this woman together with him , who was then a little girl ; he , in his destitution , by chance first made application

sections.label: Section 5.4.925
sections.translation: to the father of Chrysis —

sections.label: Section 5.4.925b
sections.translation: He ’ s beginning his tale .

sections.label: Section 5.4.925c
sections.translation: Let him alone .

sections.label: Section 5.4.926
sections.translation: Really , is he to be interrupting me in this way ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.926b
sections.translation: Do you proceed .

sections.label: Section 5.4.926c
sections.translation: He who received him was a relation of mine . There I heard from him that he was a native of Attica . He died there .

sections.label: Section 5.4.928b
sections.translation: His name ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.928c
sections.translation: The name , in such a hurry !

sections.label: Section 5.4.928d
sections.translation: Phania .

sections.label: Section 5.4.929
sections.translation: Hah ! I shall die !

sections.label: Section 5.4.929b
sections.original: Verum hercle opinor fuisse Phaniam : hoc certo scio
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really think it was Phania ; this I know for certain ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.930
sections.translation: he said that he was a citizen of Rhamnus . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.930b
sections.translation: O Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 5.4.930c
sections.translation: Many other persons in Andros have heard the same , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.4.931b
sections.original: Utinam id sit quod spero . Eho , dic mihi ,
sections.translation: I trust it may turn out as I hope . ( To CRITO .) Come now , tell me , what did he then say about her ? Did he say she was his own daughter ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.932b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 5.4.932c
sections.translation: Whose then ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.932d
sections.translation: His brother ’ s daughter .

sections.label: Section 5.4.933
sections.translation: She certainly is mine .

sections.label: Section 5.4.933b
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.933c
sections.translation: What is this that you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.933d
sections.translation: Prick up your ears , Pamphilus .

sections.label: Section 5.4.934
sections.translation: Why do you suppose so ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.934b
sections.translation: That Phania was my brother .

sections.label: Section 5.4.934c
sections.translation: I knew him , and I am aware of it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.935
sections.original: Is hinc bellum fugiens , meque in Asiam persequens , proficiscitur .
sections.translation: He , flying from the wars , and following me to Asia , set out from here . At the same time he was afraid to leave her here behind ; since then , this is the first time I have heard what became of him .

sections.label: Section 5.4.937b
sections.original: Vix sum apud me , ita animus commotus est metu ,
sections.translation: I am scarcely myself , so much has my mind been agitated by fear , hope , joy , and surprise at this so great , so unexpected blessing .

sections.label: Section 5.4.939
sections.original: Nae istam multimodis tuam inveniri gaudeo .
sections.translation: Really , I am glad for many reasons that she has been discovered to be a citizen .

sections.label: Section 5.4.939b
sections.translation: I believe it , father .

sections.label: Section 5.4.940
sections.original: At mihi unus scrupulus etiam restat qui me male habet .
sections.translation: But there yet remains one difficulty [*] with me , which keeps me in suspense .

sections.label: Section 5.4.940b
sections.translation: You deserve to be —, with your scruples , you plague . You are seeking a knot in a bulrush . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.941b
sections.translation: What is that ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.942
sections.translation: The names don ’ t agree .

sections.label: Section 5.4.942b
sections.translation: Troth , she had another when little .

sections.label: Section 5.4.942c
sections.translation: What was it , Crito ? Can you remember it ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.943b
sections.translation: I ’ m trying to recollect it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.943c
sections.translation: Am I to suffer his memory to stand in the way of my happiness , when I myself can provide my own remedy in this matter ? I will not suffer it . ( Aloud .) Hark you , Chremes , that which you are trying to recollect is Pasibula .

sections.label: Section 5.4.945b
sections.translation: The very same .

sections.label: Section 5.4.945c
sections.translation: That ’ s it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.946
sections.translation: I ’ ve heard it from herself a thousand times .

sections.label: Section 5.4.946b
sections.translation: I suppose , Chremes , that you believe that we all rejoice at this discovery .
